channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Spin-polarized light-emitting diodes based on CrI₃ operating without external spin injection
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 13 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71743-y

This study shows spin-LEDs based on a 2D magnet that emit circularly polarized light with helicity controlled by its magnetic order without the need for external spin injectors, offering a simple, integrable platform for future spin-optoelectronics.
item.dc:title: Spin-polarized light-emitting diodes based on CrI₃ operating without external spin injection
item.dc:creator: Chung-Chun Lu
Li-Wei Chang
Wei-Qing Li
Po-Liang Chen
Yen-Ju Lin
Kun-Hung Pan
Ching Kuo
Chang-Hua Liu

item.title: Reconfigurable skin electronics enabled by intrinsically stretchable photoelectric memory transistors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 13 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71589-4

Logic-in-memory is a promising approach to break the von Neumann bottleneck in conventional skin electronics. Park et al. report a wafer-scale stretchable photoelectric memory transistor array that integrates non-volatile memory with in-situ logic reconfiguration, enabling stable operation even under strain.
item.dc:title: Reconfigurable skin electronics enabled by intrinsically stretchable photoelectric memory transistors
item.dc:creator: Seon Hoo Park
Min Woo Jeong
Ngoc Thanh Phuong Vo
Hye Rin Chang
Thuy An Nguyen
Sung Yeon Hwang
Seung Hwan Lee
Tae Il Lee
Jin Young Oh

item.title: A thousand-state optoelectronic memory for high-precision spatiotemporal encoding
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 13 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71504-x

Noise in optoelectronic memories causes harmful overlap of photoconductance states and degrades computing precision. Zhou et al. report an optoelectronic memory that achieves thousands of distinguishable states without denoising, enabling the detection of fast-moving objects.
item.dc:title: A thousand-state optoelectronic memory for high-precision spatiotemporal encoding
item.dc:creator: Guangdong Zhou
Yu Xu
Xuesen Xie
Chaoyi Zhu
Jin Ye
Yang Chai

item.title: Electronic band and core-shell structure engineering enables ultrahigh energy storage in high-entropy ceramics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71892-0

A high-entropy strategy combines flattened electronic bands, core–shell microstructures, and polymorphic polar phases to overcome the trade-off between polarization and breakdown electric field, achieving high energy storage performance in lead-free dielectric ceramics.
item.dc:title: Electronic band and core-shell structure engineering enables ultrahigh energy storage in high-entropy ceramics
item.dc:creator: Yunting Li
Peng Li
Haihua Huang
Hairui Bai
Fei Xu
Jigong Hao
Peng Fu
Juan Du
Zhongbin Pan
Wangfeng Bai
Wei Li
Jiwei Zhai
Zhenxiang Cheng
